Getting started

How to run Prism locally, where desktop distribution is headed, and what privacy defaults to expect.

Home / Articles
ArticleFor everyone getting-starteddistributionprivacy

Summary

How to run Prism locally, where desktop distribution is headed, and what privacy defaults to expect.

Article

Recommended Local Run

For a full local stack, copy .env.example to .env, set the required secrets, and run Docker Compose. The web app opens at http://localhost:18788; API health is at http://localhost:18787/health.

Local Development

For source development, install the shared packages, config package, API, and web dependencies, pull the default Ollama models, then run npm run dev from the repo root. The root README lists the exact commands.

Private By Default

Prism binds to loopback by default, so other devices on the network cannot connect unless network sharing is explicitly enabled. LOCAL mode should keep chat, auxiliary work, embeddings, and online-only image generation within the local privacy boundary.

Desktop Distribution

Official desktop builds are free. GitHub Releases are the baseline delivery path for macOS, Windows, and Linux artifacts, with optional Patreon support treated as patronage rather than product access.

Launch Caveat

The product-worthy launch checklist is still pending. Broad public promotion should wait until platform artifacts, first-run setup, LOCAL privacy, support copy, and outreach materials have recorded pass evidence.
